meta.desc: Pertubuhan Amal & Da'wah Anak Istimewa (PANDAI) — a registered Islamic NGO empowering special-needs children, families and communities through compassion, knowledge and da'wah.

home.heroTitle: Caring for Special-Needs Children with Compassion, Knowledge and Da'wah
home.heroLead: Special-needs children and their families need structured, friendly and continuous support. PANDAI builds that ecosystem of support — together with families, communities and institutions.
home.introText: Pertubuhan Amal & Da'wah Anak Istimewa (PANDAI) is an Islamic NGO registered since 26 June 2018 under the welfare category (disability sub-category). We focus on the areas closest to the real needs of special-needs children and their families.
home.focusLead: Each focus area complements the others to build a more complete ecosystem of support.

focus.title: Family Support
focus.desc: Guidance, emotional support and a community network for parents.

focus.title: Education & Awareness
focus.desc: Knowledge sharing, training and advocacy for the wider public.

focus.title: Special-Needs-Friendly Islamic Programmes
focus.desc: Activities that bring children closer to worship, good character and a compassionate environment.

focus.title: Community & Volunteers
focus.desc: Mobilising community involvement, volunteers and strategic partners.

home.autismLead: PANDAI has been appointed as the operator of the MAIWP Autism Centre — an initiative to serve autistic children and their families professionally and compassionately.

values.name: Rahmah
values.desc: Caring with love, wisdom and empathy.

values.name: Inclusion
values.desc: Opening spaces that are welcoming and accepting.

values.name: Dignity
values.desc: Honouring the dignity of every person.

about.title: About Us
about.lead: A registered Islamic NGO since 26 June 2018, welfare category (disability sub-category).
about.missionText: To build support grounded in compassion, knowledge and da'wah to empower special-needs children, families and communities.
about.issueLead: Special-needs children and their families need support that is more structured, friendly and continuous.

issues.title: Parents Need Guidance
issues.desc: Information, emotional support and practical guidance are often needed in the journey of raising a special-needs child.

issues.title: Society Needs Understanding
issues.desc: Awareness and proper etiquette in interaction must be strengthened so communities become more empathetic and inclusive.

issues.title: Places of Worship Must Be Inclusive
issues.desc: Mosques and suraus need to be better prepared to welcome special-needs children and their families.

about.issueOutro: These needs call for synergy between families, communities, institutions and da'wah support.
about.modelTitle: Our Approach: Aql, Nafs, Qalb, Ruh
about.modelLead: Our approach sees the special-needs child as a whole — mind, behaviour, emotions and spirit. This model helps shape interventions that are balanced and compassionate.

model.name: Qalb
model.desc: Emotions: a sense of safety, relationships and wellbeing.

model.name: Ruh
model.desc: Spirit: values, worship, prayer and meaning in life.

about.valuesOutro: These values guide every initiative and collaboration we undertake.

programs.title: Programmes & Activities
programs.lead: Our programmes are designed to serve special-needs children, families and communities in practical and continuous ways.

items.title: Special-Needs Classes
items.desc: Learning activities, character building and self-development for special-needs children in a safe, friendly environment.

items.title: Parent Support
items.desc: Guidance, shared experiences and a supportive community for parents and caregivers.

items.title: Talks & Awareness
items.desc: Public education towards empathy and understanding of special-needs children.

items.title: Ziarah Kasih (Care Visits)
items.desc: Reaching out to families of special-needs children with support and care at their homes.

items.title: Volunteering
items.desc: Mobilising volunteers for meaningful service to the community.

items.title: Mosque & Surau Collaboration
items.desc: Making places of worship more inclusive and welcoming for special-needs children and families.

programs.outro: Programmes are shaped by real needs on the ground and a spirit of service.
programs.joinText: Contact us for programme registration or to become a volunteer.

autism.title: MAIWP Autism Centre
autism.lead: PANDAI has been appointed as the operator of the MAIWP Autism Centre.
autism.aboutText: The MAIWP Autism Centre is an initiative of the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP) to provide support services for autistic children and their families. As the appointed operator, PANDAI is responsible for the centre's daily operations with a professional and compassionate approach.
autism.roleText: As operator, PANDAI brings years of experience serving special-needs children into the management of the centre — from intervention programmes and family support to staff training and community awareness.

services.title: Intervention Programmes
services.desc: Structured activities for the development of autistic children based on individual needs.

services.title: Family Support
services.desc: Guidance and support spaces for parents and caregivers of autistic children.

services.title: Islamic Approach
services.desc: Learning worship, character and good values adapted to the needs of autistic children.

services.title: Community Awareness
services.desc: Advocacy and training programmes to help society better understand autism.

autism.visitText: For enquiries about services, registration or visits to the MAIWP Autism Centre, please contact us.

news.title: News & Activities
news.lead: Follow the latest updates on PANDAI's programmes and activities.
news.empty: No news at the moment. Please check back later.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.lead: Meaningful moments from our programmes and activities.

calendar.title: Activity Calendar
calendar.empty: No activities scheduled at the moment.

donate.title: Make a Donation
donate.lead: Every contribution helps us continuously care for special-needs children and their families.
donate.noteText: After making a donation, you may send your payment proof to our email for record purposes. May Allah reward your generosity.

usage.title: Special-Needs Programmes
usage.desc: Classes, activities and learning materials for special-needs children.

usage.title: Family Support
usage.desc: Guidance programmes and care visits to families in need.

usage.title: Operations & Awareness
usage.desc: Mobilising volunteers and community awareness programmes.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.lead: Questions about programmes, volunteering or the MAIWP Autism Centre? We are happy to help.

footer.aboutText: A registered Islamic NGO empowering special-needs children, families and communities through compassion, knowledge and da'wah.
